title: GAIA SOTA-pursuit: fix max-turns CLI bug + cost controls

haiku_4_5.note: 1 false positive (17000 vs 17, substr judge bug)

top_priority_fixes.title: Fix max-turns CLI hardcode
top_priority_fixes.rationale: iter-22 raised DEFAULT_MAX_TURNS to 12 in gaia-agent.ts but gaia-bench.ts CLI hardcodes '8'. At least 6 Sonnet questions failed at exactly turns=8 with null answer. Fix is 1 character change.

top_priority_fixes.title: Web browse output truncation
top_priority_fixes.rationale: Sonnet cost $4.97 vs $0.36 for Haiku — 14x more. Web browse returns verbose HTML content. Truncate to 4000 chars max. Target: Sonnet <$1.50 per 53-Q run.

top_priority_fixes.title: GAIA attachment download + serve
top_priority_fixes.rationale: ~8-10 L1 questions require Excel/PDF/image attachments. These are unanswerable without files. Download from HF gaia-benchmark dataset (requires HF_TOKEN). Serve via file_read or python_exec.

top_priority_fixes.title: Fix substring judge false positives
top_priority_fixes.rationale: answer='17000' matched expected='17' via substring. Judge should require exact numeric match or normalized float comparison, not substring. Also affects multi-word answers.

sonnet_passes_iter23: answer=FunkMonk (iter-21 websearch fallback paid off, 8 turns)
answer=broccoli, celery, fresh basil, lettuce, sweet potatoes (grocery, 6 turns)
answer=THE SEAGULL GLIDED PEACEFULLY TO MY CHAIR (cipher, 4 turns)
start_command: git checkout bench/adr-133-sota-meta && git checkout -b feat/iter-24-max-turns-fix
run_command: node v3/@claude-flow/cli/bin/cli.js gaia-bench run --level 1 --limit 53 --models claude-haiku-4-5,claude-sonnet-4-6 --concurrency 6 --max-turns 12 --output json 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gaia-l1-iter24.json
